what:
An Language Package for Facebook Messenger API. The adaptor lets OpenFn workflows read from and write to Meta Graph API (Facebook Messenger / Pages).

It exposes 1 operation that you can call from a workflow step, listed in the Operations tab. 5 ready-to-paste snippets are grouped as create (5). Vendor homepage: https://developers.facebook.com.
connect:
Meta supports OAuth 2.0 or bearer tokens for API access.

The credential schema asks for access_token (required). Point the credential at the base URL: https://graph.facebook.com/v21.0. Auth reference: https://developers.facebook.com/docs/facebook-login/guides/access-tokens.
when:
Reach for this adaptor when your workflow needs to read or write data in Meta Graph API (Facebook Messenger / Pages).

4 triggers are catalogued (1 webhook, 3 polling), including New Comment on Post, New Lead, New Post by Others on Page, New Post by You. See the Triggers tab for the full list. Meta can push events to OpenFn via webhooks (docs: https://developers.facebook.com/docs/graph-api/webhooks), so reactive workflows are a good fit.
wire:
A typical wiring pattern is trigger → fetch → transform → write.

Kick the workflow off from a trigger on the Triggers tab (webhook-driven where supported, polling otherwise). Then chain operations from this adaptor's Operations tab to fetch or write data in Meta. Pass values between steps through state.data — every operation reads from and writes to job state.

To move data between systems, follow this step with another adaptor (e.g. write the fetched records into a downstream FHIR, DHIS2, or database adaptor).
scale:
When running Meta jobs at volume:

- Prefer Meta webhooks over polling — they cut latency and rate-limit pressure.
- Paginate list and search calls explicitly; never assume a single response is the full result set.
- Make jobs idempotent by keying on stable external IDs so a replay doesn't create duplicates.
- Surface 4xx/5xx responses with the vendor request ID in your logs, and treat 429s as retries with backoff.

Adapter note: Graph API is versioned (~quarterly). Adapter focus appears to be Messenger Send API + Page webhooks.
best:
- Store Meta credentials in OpenFn's credential store; never inline keys or tokens in job code.
- Pin @openfn/language-facebook to v0.4.29 in package.json so an upstream change doesn't break a production workflow silently.
- Keep each step small — one logical API call per operation makes failures easier to diagnose and retry.
- Add defensive checks before transforming inbound payloads — vendor responses change between versions.
- Log enough context (resource id, run id, vendor request id) so an incident can be traced end-to-end.
